welcomeModal.title: // welcome to hypar
welcomeModal.subtitle: Walk through six short steps to wire providers, your vector store, embeddings, chunking, search, and chat — then you are ready to talk to your documents.

features.providers: Pick models and API keys that match how you run locally or in the cloud.
features.vector: Point the app at Postgres + pgvector or another compatible store.
features.layers: Each screen explains what that layer does so nothing feels like a black box.

chat.title: chat with your knowledge base
chat.subtitle: lightweight agent: chooses whether to search your documents (RAG) or reply without the vector store.
chat.empty: ask something about the documents you uploaded. follow-up questions keep context.
chat.errorGeneric: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
chat.errorUnreachable: Cannot reach the model. Is Ollama running?
chat.errorUnauthorized: API key invalid or missing. Check your provider configuration.
chat.errorRateLimit: Rate limit reached. Please wait a moment and try again.
chat.noModelReply: The model searched your knowledge base but did not return any reply text (common with very small local models). Try sending your message again, switch to a larger chat model, or open **show retrieval** below to read the passages that were retrieved.

commands.remember: save a piece of information to your local memory. E.g.: /remember my favorite color is orange
commands.forget: delete memories containing a text. E.g.: /forget favorite color
commands.list: show everything saved in your local memory.
commands.search: search your knowledge base without using the model. E.g.: /search kubernetes config

chat.errorReach: could not reach the api at {url}. is the backend running?
chat.errorHint: with docker: `docker compose up -d --build backend frontend` then hard-refresh.
chat.searchedHint: the agent invoked the knowledge-base search tool
chat.citedHint: the answer references retrieved passages with [n]

documents.title: documents
documents.emptyHint: upload your first document to get started
documents.confirmDelete: delete "{title}"? its chunks and embeddings will be removed.

document.chunksHint: (the pieces that get embedded and searched)

upload.title: add document
upload.subtitle: paste text or upload a file. we'll chunk and embed it for retrieval.
upload.fieldContentPlaceholder: paste your text or notes here…
upload.dropZone: drag and drop files here, or click to browse
upload.errorIngestion: Processing failed. Try re-chunking from the document page.
upload.successHint: Your document has been chunked and indexed in the vector database.

settings.title: // runtime settings
settings.subtitle: Overrides .env values without restart. Priority: DB > .env > default.
settings.savedOk: {section} saved. Changes apply immediately; no restart needed.
settings.savedFail: Could not save. Check your admin token and try again.
settings.resetOk: Reset to .env values; database overrides were cleared.
settings.resetFail: Could not reset. Check your token and try again.

chrome.applyRuntimeSuccess: Settings saved. The server reads these values from the database now (same override path as the admin panel).
chrome.applyRuntimeError: Could not apply settings. Try again or use download .env.
chrome.runtimeNoticeTitle: How this wizard relates to the running app
chrome.runtimeNoticeBody: Values are kept in your browser and optionally synced to this server while no admin account exists yet. The Node server does not read localStorage: it uses environment variables and, after you click apply, the same database overrides the admin panel uses (DB > .env > defaults). Downloading .env is still the right path for another machine, Docker, or CI. If you change DATABASE_URL, restart the app and set the variable in the real environment — Prisma does not reconnect from the wizard alone.
chrome.stepAddEnv: Download `.env` to the project root, or use Apply to push settings into this server (API keys and tuning take effect without a restart).
chrome.stepRestart: If you changed the database URL, restart Nuxt or your containers and set DATABASE_URL in the host or compose environment.
chrome.stepRunStack: Install dependencies and start the stack
chrome.storageInfo: writes -> localStorage + server sync (before first admin)

intro.title: onboarding wizard
intro.subtitle: Configure your local RAG stack and generate `.env`. Then jump to challenges to implement embeddings, chunking, and retrieval.

apis.title: API Configuration
apis.subtitle: Connect embedding and generation providers
apis.what:
Your RAG needs two services:
- **Embedding provider** to convert text into vectors
- **LLM provider** to generate final answers
apis.why:
Using provider abstraction keeps your stack flexible.
You can swap models later without rewriting the wizard or `.env` contract.
apis.codeExplanation: These environment variables define active providers and their credentials.

vectorDb.title: Vector Database
vectorDb.subtitle: Configure your vector store — self-hosted pgvector or Supabase
vectorDb.what:
Postgres with **pgvector** extension stores and searches vectors by semantic similarity.
Choose between **self-hosted** (Docker) or **Supabase** (hosted pgvector — no infra needed).
vectorDb.why:
Traditional databases match exact words. Vector databases match **meaning**:
"deployment" can find "hosting" or "production setup".
vectorDb.codeExplanation: Postgres with pgvector preinstalled. Ports and credentials come from `.env`.

embeddings.title: Embedding Service
embeddings.subtitle: Convert text into semantic vectors
embeddings.what:
Service that transforms text into vectors.
Each vector represents the **meaning** of the text.
embeddings.why:
Keyword search matches exact words. Embedding search understands intent:
"machine learning tutorial" can match "AI guide" even without shared words.
embeddings.codeExplanation: Calls an embedding endpoint and returns the vector output.

chunking.title: Document Processing
chunking.subtitle: Chunk and store your documents with embeddings
chunking.what:
Ingestion pipeline:
1. **Split** documents into chunks
2. **Generate** embeddings per chunk
3. **Store** everything in the vector DB
chunking.why:
LLMs have limited context windows. Smaller chunks improve retrieval precision.
**Overlap** prevents context loss between chunk boundaries.
chunking.codeExplanation: Splits content into overlapping chunks to preserve context continuity.

search.title: Similarity Search
search.subtitle: Find relevant chunks using vector similarity
search.what: Search pipeline finds the most relevant chunks for a question using vector similarity.
search.why:
Question -> query embedding -> nearest chunks -> top-K context.
Cosine similarity measures semantic closeness.
search.codeExplanation: The `<=>` operator computes cosine distance. Lower is more similar.

rag.title: RAG Complete!
rag.subtitle: Combine retrieval and generation for grounded answers
rag.what: **Retrieval-Augmented Generation** retrieves context and injects it into the prompt before generation.
rag.why:
Without RAG, LLM answers rely only on training data.
With RAG, answers are grounded in **your documents**.
rag.codeExplanation: Retrieval + generation: the model answers using retrieved context.

ollamaBaseUrl.helpText: Base URL for local Ollama instance

supabaseProjectRef.label: Project reference
supabaseProjectRef.helpText: Found in Supabase dashboard → Project Settings → General (e.g. abcdefghijklmnopqrst)

supabaseDbPassword.label: Database password
supabaseDbPassword.helpText: Found in Supabase dashboard → Project Settings → Database → Database password

supabasePoolMode.label: Connection mode
supabasePoolMode.helpText: Use 'Direct' for migrations and low-concurrency. Use 'Transaction pooler' for serverless / high-concurrency.
